scenario: Client Hub Check-In · why we capture baseline telemetry before load
script: Before we touch a weight, we need your baseline. If your wearable isn't synced yet, log your sleep and stress manually right here. The engine needs this data to calculate your central nervous system readiness. Based on what you log, the Recovery Prescription below will automatically adapt to fix your specific pain points and prep your joints for today’s load. Give the system the data, and it will give you the blueprint.

scenario: Program tab · Enter the Floor · how to read rep ranges and RPE
script: Step three: Enter the floor. Look closely at the structure today—pay attention to the rep ranges and the RPE. RPE is your Rate of Perceived Exertion on a scale of one to ten. It dictates how heavy you should go. An RPE of eight means you should only have two reps left in the tank. Do not just go through the motions. Match the prescribed intensity, hit the target sets, and earn your rest.

scenario: Post-Workout Check-In modal · capture pain + RPE to feed the recovery engine
script: Great work on the floor today. Before you log off, we need to capture your post-workout data. Take a second to scan your body. Do you have any new target areas of pain or friction? Log the joint and rate the pain from one to ten. Finally, lock in your overall session difficulty. This feedback loops right back into the engine to prep your recovery for tomorrow. Lock it in, save, and recover.

scenario: Nutrition Locker · TDEE-engineered macros, interchangeable meals, adherence payoff
script: Listen — this meal plan is not a guess. Every number on that macro wheel was reverse-engineered from your TDEE, your total daily energy expenditure. We did the math on exactly what your body burns, then built your protein, carbs, and fat around it so the fuel matches the mission. Now hear this — the meals are interchangeable. You don't have to eat it exactly as written. Swap one meal for another, eat last night's leftovers, build your own plate — as long as you measure it right and it hits your macros, the success still comes. The magic isn't the recipe; it's the numbers. So follow the targets to the T, weigh your food, log every meal, protein first, water on point — do that, and the results aren't a maybe, they're mathematics. Respect the work that went into this, trust the plan, and just Believe. That's how you build the best version of your body.

scenario: Post-Workout Check-In · Recovery Prescription card · what it is and why it appears (deterministically built from the check-in's pain + RPE + flagged target area)
script: This right here is your Recovery Prescription, and it is not random. The second you logged your check-in, the engine read two things: the joint you flagged, and how hard that session actually hit you. Then it built tomorrow's plan around exactly that — the right movements for that specific area, dialed to the right volume. See the verdict up top? Regress means we back off to protect the joint. Maintain means we hold steady with a measured load. Progress means you earned the green light to push. Every drill below is hand-picked to bulletproof the spot that needs it, and we finish with a breathing reset to lock the recovery in. Nothing here is popping up by accident — this is the system working for you around the clock. Trust the protocol, run it, and Believe.
